templates.title: HealthLog
templates.name: healthlog
templates.description: Self-hosted personal health tracker (PWA + native iOS client): weight, blood pressure, pulse, blood glucose, body composition, sleep, mood, menstrual cycle, and medications on one encrypted timeline. AES-256-GCM at rest, no vendor cloud, no telemetry. Ships app + PostgreSQL 16 as one stack.
templates.note: Generate each secret with: openssl rand -hex 32 (a 64-char hex string). For plain-HTTP / LAN access set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE to false, or login fails. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL and APP_URL to how you reach the app. Full guide: https://github.com/MBombeck/HealthLog/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/getting-started.md

env.name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
env.label: PostgreSQL password
env.description: Database password. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

env.name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
env.label: Encryption key (data at rest)
env.description: AES-256-GCM key. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Back this up — losing it makes encrypted data unrecoverable.

env.name: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE
env.label: Session cookie Secure flag
env.description: Set to false for plain-HTTP / LAN access, or login fails. Set to true only behind an HTTPS reverse proxy.

env.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
env.label: Public app URL
env.description: How users reach HealthLog, e.g. http://nas:3000 or https://healthlog.example.com

env.name: APP_URL
env.label: Internal app URL
env.description: Normally identical to the public app URL.
